channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Self-Verified Distillation: Your Language Model Is Secretly Its Own Synthetic Data Pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2605.26132v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can post-trained large language models (LLMs) further improve themselves using only unlabeled prompts, without external teachers or feedback from tools? We study this setting starting only from unlabeled seed questions with no ground-truth solutions, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, prompt, tool, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pretraining Data Exposure in Large Language Models: A Survey of Membership Inference, Data Contamination, and Security Implications
item.description: arXiv:2605.26133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become the predominant paradigm in NLP, advancing both research and industry. As model sizes and pretraining data grow, concerns about Pretraining Data Exposure (PDE) increase due to the scale and opacity of training ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, paper, LLM, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CroCo: Cross-Lingual Contrastive Preference Tuning on Self-Generations
item.description: arXiv:2605.26293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work establishes that controlled contrastiveness between self-generated responses from large language models, set via reward scores, improves downstream preference tuning in English. We extend this method to multiple languages and evaluate two m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, model, arxiv, fine-tuning, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Memory Architectures for Multi-Turn Text-to-SQL: A Benchmark and Empirical Study
item.description: arXiv:2605.26394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn Text-to-SQL is central to enterprise analytics yet remains predominantly evaluated in single-turn settings. We introduce EnterpriseMem-Bench, a multi-turn Text-to-SQL benchmark of 300 sessions and 1,400 turns built programmatically from thr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, memory, reasoning, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check
item.description: arXiv:2605.26242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can large language models detect and report their own internal states? A number of studies have argued that the answer to this question is yes. We argue, based on lessons from human metacognition research, that this conclusion may be premature: to be ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, in-context, research, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Experiments in Agentic AI for Science
item.description: arXiv:2605.26305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper details two novel frameworks for developing autonomous, agentic AI in scientific workflows. Both systems leverage a hybrid Local Body, Remote Brain architecture via Google Colab, utilizing Python-based local orchestrators to invoke large la...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, paper, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OmniToM: Benchmarking Theory of Mind in LLMs via Explicit Belief Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.26322v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM), the ability to infer others' knowledge, intentions, and emotions, is commonly evaluated in large language models (LLMs) using end-point question answering, where performance is judged solely by the final answer to a social reason...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, augmented, LLM, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Planning Neural Dynamics with Lie Group Embedding through Supervised Projective Manifold Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.26167v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose Lie group embedded dynamical neural networks (LieEDNN) and the corresponding learning algorithms based on gradient descent and metric projection on smooth manifold, where we treat Lie group as an intrinsic representation for continuous symm...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, vector, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
item.description: arXiv:2605.26182v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating physically buildable brick structures from 3D shapes requires more than geometric reconstruction: the output must also satisfy discrete part constraints and structural stability. Existing brick generation methods either rely on heuristic op...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, framework, model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Constraint Tax: Measuring Validity-Correctness Tradeoffs in Structured Outputs for Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.26128v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Production LLM systems increasingly require machine-readable outputs: JSON objects, typed traces, regex-constrained fields, and tool-call schemas. This paper targets on-device and low-cost small language model (SLM) deployments, where sub-3B models ar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, tool, paper, LLM, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neural Bayesian Sequential Routing
item.description: arXiv:2605.26147v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human decision-making is sequential and uncertainty-aware, yet standard neural networks often rely on static, dense forward computation with limited visibility into evidence acquisition, uncertainty evolution, or when computation should stop. We intro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, vector, model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TSFMAudit: Data Contamination Auditing in Forecasting Time Series Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.26161v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series foundation models (TSFMs) are increasingly pretrained on large corpora, raising concerns that evaluation datasets may have been exposed during pretraining and thus yield overly optimistic performance estimates. Auditing such contamination ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, vision, LLM, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Push-Based Asynchronous Federated Learning: A Bias-Correction Aggregation Approach
item.description: arXiv:2605.26162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Asynchronous decentralized federated learning (ADFL) eliminates central coordination and global synchronization, making it attractive for large-scale and heterogeneous systems. However, frequent peer-to-peer communication, asynchronous updates on dire...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, framework, RAG, compression, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Constraint acquisition needs better benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2605.26279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Constraint Acquisition (CA) and related research on the validation and enhancement of Mathematical Programming (MP) models from domain knowledge artifacts are currently limited by inadequate benchmarks. This deficiency impedes reproducibility and cros...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, research, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks — by Artificial Analysis and IBM
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)
